reviews.review_msg: Professor Archibald has to be one of the most chill teachers in the math department. Lectures don't feel repetitive and have an element of nuance to them. There are cool examples, other times, he will accidentally refresh the page and redo the notes at hyper speed! He uses slides that he fills in notes on and is easy to follow along.
After finishing this semester, I've known Dr. Archibald to be very funny and chill. If you study well and practice with textbook questions and practice exams, you're definitely gonna do good.

Great prof, his prac exams are almost identical to the real exams which is really helpful. He is very helpful and fair. In the tuts all we do are prac worksheets which is helpful! Overall, if you have a chance, def take it w him.

Tom is awsome. He is a very fair examiner. The practice midterms and the study questions he posts on canvas for the quizzes are your best friends to prepare for the MTs. The exam questions are always like the easier of the assigned textbook questions. Overall super fair and if you put in the work, you will do well. Thoroughly recommend.

In math 110 right now with him. He's actually pretty nice and makes the math we learn straight forward. He doesn't trick you on exams too. Just study and participate in class, hoping to end this sem with an A. Be wary, he's very against cheating and can get mad if the class doesn't follow his instructions. Just practice and you're going to do good.

hes really chill, some of his methods are a little outdated but you can easily just go on youtube to figure some things out. quizzes are free marks, just beware of midterms and finals. other than that, not a big course to stress on. cool guy

T Archie is the BEST! Makes math FUN! 8:30 lectures are awful but he makes life worth living. Weekly quizzes but only 2 questions, lots of chances to boost your mark. If you need to take math, take it with him! He's quirky and witty and loves his history. He's been doing this a long time so he gets it and he understands the student life!
an amazing prof, super understanding and easy going!

As someone who is learning most of the content for the first time, Archie does not explain what is going on in anyway that makes sense for me. He gets example questions wrong and doesnt correct them, which is a pain in the ass for someone who doesnt understand. He doesnt upload complete notes so review is nearly impossible using only course content

I had Tom for MATH 260: Introduction To Differential Equations and he was a great prof. He is very clear on what will be tested for the midterms and has interactive lecture notes and Participation Quizzes in tutorials (forces you to go). Besides that, he tries his best to make the content interesting relating it to real-life situations when viable.
Overall pretty solid for a math prof. Had him first semester and was nice for it to not be too material heavy.

One of the greatest profs I have ever met at SFU. This 380W class was really fascinating if you have a moderate interest in history. The algebraic and geometric questions are fairly straightforward like high-school level. It's an upper-level writing course but Tom does not require you to have that advanced writing skills, which is nice for us.

Easily the worst math teacher I've ever had. Barely explains how to do the material and didn't really answer questions in a helpful way. He might be a better teacher in-person, but online was a disaster. I basically had to teacher myself the course material.

MATH154 is a repeat of what you learn in Calculus 12 so it's a breeze if you've taken that. Tom himself is a pretty good teacher. He gets the point across but, sometimes is not super straightforward, so you can get confused along the way.

This man went too lightly with godly. slug slayer 3000 and hands down such a funny prof. Overall math 154 isn't too difficult, tom does explain things in a more complex way but if you put ur mind to it, you'll understand him. Do not cheat as they do send out warnings to people who have cheated. We will never forget you Godly
Expect to learn most of the material on your own. Lecture was really dull and confusing. Overall an understanding and generous professor.

I like how he weighed the course, 50% was zoom polls and homework so lots of free marks and pretty hard to fail. The average was high - 76%. The exams are similar to the written homework he gives. Personally, I don't think he is good at teaching, often skips steps and his writing is very messy but he's kind and understanding.
The class was ok I guess, you'll be ok if you do all the homework and look at the study guides for what's going on the exams.

Professor Archibald is a great teacher. He created pre-lecture content so you have an idea of what will be taught each lecture and will have an easier time following. Homework and tests were very fair and connected! As someone who often struggles with math and would receive low grades, I've done so well in this course and it's all thanks to Tom!
Bring a magnifying glass to read profs chicken scratch writing
this guy is so adorable bro

Professor Archibald is honestly one of the best professors I've had at SFU. You can clearly see his passion about math through his teaching. He made one of the midterms and the final on the tougher side, but I think he helps a lot with the homework. His lectures were straight from the textbook, but his explanations were very thorough.

Tom is an amazing person! Super kind, considerate, and has hilarious catchphrases. With that being said, his lectures are boring and not worth the time. His notes are extremely confusing and really hard to follow. Be prepared to watch youtube videos and teach yourself.

Professor Tom was a very nice person. He seems to put his best work in every lecture to explain the materials. It is the politeness and the respectful environment he created that inspired and motivated me to join his class. It was very common to study some materials by myself, and when I was confused professor Tom was quick to answer my question.
Mediocre prof. Lecture notes are confusing. Prof makes the content more confusing than it should be. Just watch YouTube videos

As a professor, Tom really changed my life. His course made me realize my strengths in math and he is very accommodating and encouraging. He cares about his students and that really made me respect him. The course requires some strong writing skills, unlike most math classes, but Tom is always there to help. Thank you, Tom.
My man. Best math prof.

Really easy to talk to, and super nice guy. And, he's had a solid background teaching mathematics in China(?) and also some part in Europe(?) Also very passionate about his field as demonstrated in the interesting, and funny stories he ties into the lectures related to math.
Good prof. went through the material pretty fast but gave good explanations of everything. You can skip a few classes here and there but don't miss too many or you will fall behind.

His course is very simple, you have the skeleton notes and you can fill it in during class. There are weekly online assignments that are simple, and weekly quizzes that are simple. Overall straight forward class, Tom himself is a great person.

Tom Archibald is a solid choice for mathematics. His lectures are engaging and he is open to questions during the lectures. He is clear and explains concepts well. His office hours are fairly open, and they are well worth going to. He is very kind-hearted and fair.
Very approachable and friendly prof. Ask lots of questions!

Tom is a really caring and responsible professor. If you have a question, you should go to his office and he will be glad and he will help you to get a good grade. His class is not hard, you just need to go to class and take notes cuz he wont post them. Its quite easy to get a good grade if you keep in touch with him.
class avg C-, unbelievable. Have no idea what he was teaching in class. Even his TA's teaching skill is better.

Pretty dull lectures. Weekly quizzes with questions taken straight from the homework assigned for this week to encourage you to study systematically. Midterms were straightforward, final was a bit more challenging, but the grades get heavily scaled in the end.
Tom is the reason I started to love math ;)

Math 380 is really hard, so buckle up. However, Tom really cares about his students so if you can make it through the innumerable assignments and difficult exams you'll come out with a newfound respect for mathematics. He also takes the time to try and learn his student's names, which I thought was important at the university level. A great guy!
good guy
ONE OF THE BEST PROF IN SFU I EVER HAD. IT'S WORTH TO TAKE HIS CLASS. REALLY CARES ABOUT STUDENT, GIVE EXTRA HELP. ALTHOUGH THE CLASS IS NOT EASY, BUT YOU WILL SURVIVE AND ACTUALLY LEARN SOMETHING.

I've never rated a prof on this site before but out of my two years at sfu, he's been my favorite. He genuinely cares about his students and is the most hilarious math prof ever. Made 8:30 am classes worth it, even though I skipped a lot of classes near the end of the semester lol
Best prof i have met so far.
really helpful and great professor.

Great professor. He's understanding, accommodating and helpful (very accessible for extra help!)... Takes the time to remember every students name, which reflects his dedication to his class and students.

Follows text closely. This is hard material to understand, but he will clarify if you have questions. Need to read and practice on your own though, otherwise the best teacher will not help you in this course.
Very caring prof! Would def take another course with him. The W  (writing) was frustrating at times though...Assignments were weighed quite heavily and tough at times.

Excellent professor, highly recommended if you want to understand the subject. He cares for his students. My background is in economics and he helped me understand the subject. He encouraged me and offered 4 extra sessions before the final where he reviewed the material. A very intelligent professor.  Ich bin Ihnen sehr dankbar Dr.Vielen Dank
The quiz is really hard, and in midterm 1, we get a question beyond our range. He always forget to post notes on webct, but for others, he's a pretty good frof.
Great! Intelligent prof. Enthusiastic and knowledgeable about math. Lively and engaging lecture despite material being tedious at times. Recommended.

Still remains one of my favourite profs. Very considerate for individuals, lenient, fair exams and knowledgeable. Always available for extra help and encourages individual thinking outside of the box. Clear, energetic and entertaining lectures that i never fell asleep in, writing might not be the best.

First, he's teaching too fast that sometimes I have difficulties of catching his notes. Second, his quizzes are ----- 1) too difficult, 2) not much time (10 mins only) to finish. Without these, he's not a bad prof.

Personally I think Tom is a great prof. He is really responsive to questions and his lectures are really interesting if you enjoy the history of math. The assignments were a little tedious but they are few and far between. However, he does take a while to mark, or post anything. Other than that i really enjoyed the course.

useless prof... never post notes or even assignment solution on webct... not due to busy or what... but as he said "he would never do additional things that aren't get paid"... boring lectures but you must attend.. otherwise you would miss the assignment due date... this prof does change midterm day... without sending you an email... irresponsible
Very clear lecturer. Seemed very interested in the material. Gave extremely helpful suggestions with the paper.
He is too busy to post even syllabus in webct. he never post any materials(eg. assignment solution) on time. he is the one of the most idle profs in my life.
Excellent prof, very kind and responsive to students. Also has great knowlegde and able to communicate material very well.
No Comments
